semantic_description: The function count_equal_to_k scans a read-only integer array a of length n and returns the number of elements equal to scalar k. The loop is a single for-loop from i = 0 to i < n with an accumulator ret that starts at 0 and is incremented exactly when a[i] == k. The function preserves the full input array memory and supports the empty-loop case n == 0, where the result is 0.
